audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed as Job's meditation on human mortality: flower/shadow imagery, clean/unclean question, limits of days, tree hope, Sheol concealment, longing for renewal, and erosion imagery.

verses.restored: A human born of woman is short of days and full of trouble.
He comes out like a flower and withers; he flees like a shadow and does not stand.
Do you open your eyes on such a one and bring me into judgment with you?
Who can bring a clean thing out of an unclean? Not one.
Since his days are determined, the number of his months is with you; you have set his limits, and he cannot pass them.
Look away from him, that he may rest, until he enjoys like a hired worker his day.
For there is hope for a tree: if it is cut down, it will sprout again, and its shoot will not cease.
Though its root grows old in the earth and its stump dies in the dust,
at the scent of water it will bud and make branches like a young plant.
But a strong man dies and lies low; a human expires, and where is he?
Waters fail from a sea, and a river wastes away and dries up;
so a man lies down and does not rise; until the heavens are no more, they will not awake or be roused from their sleep.
If only you would hide me in Sheol, conceal me until your anger turns back, set a fixed time for me, and remember me!
If a strong man dies, will he live again? All the days of my hard service I would wait, until my change comes.
You would call, and I would answer you; you would long for the work of your hands.
For then you would count my steps; you would not keep watch over my sin.
My transgression would be sealed in a bag, and you would plaster over my guilt.
But a mountain falling crumbles, and a rock is moved from its place;
waters wear away stones; floods wash away the dust of the earth; so you destroy the hope of a mortal.
You overpower him forever, and he passes; you change his face and send him away.
His children are honored, but he does not know it; they are brought low, but he does not perceive them.
Only his flesh pains him, and his soul mourns over him.
